title: Campaign Brief
description: Structured metadata for a Kinetiks marketing campaign. Each campaign directory (./campaigns/{name}/) should have a brief.md and may optionally include a brief.json validated against this schema.

brand_id.description: Which Kinetiks brand this campaign belongs to.

campaign_name.description: Human-readable campaign name, e.g. 'Spring Product Launch 2026'.

slug.description: Kebab-case directory name for this campaign, e.g. 'spring-launch-2026'. Must match the directory name under ./campaigns/.

goal.description: What success looks like for this campaign. Should include a measurable target when possible, e.g. 'Generate 500 email signups from organic LinkedIn content in 30 days'.

created_date.description: ISO 8601 date when the campaign was created.

status.description: Current lifecycle stage of the campaign.

angle_used.description: The positioning angle driving this campaign. References ./brand/positioning.md.

name.description: Short name for the angle, e.g. 'The Anti-Course Course'.

description.description: One-paragraph explanation of the angle and why it was chosen for this campaign.

positioning_ref.description: Path to the positioning file this angle comes from, e.g. './brand/positioning.md'.

audience_segment.description: The target audience for this campaign. References ./brand/audience.md.

name.description: Segment name, e.g. 'Burned-out freelancers looking to productize'.

description.description: Detailed description of this audience segment.

audience_ref.description: Path to the audience file, e.g. './brand/audience.md'.

end_date.description: When the campaign ends or is evaluated.

channels.description: Marketing channels this campaign will use.

assets.description: Assets created for or used by this campaign.

name.description: Human-readable name for this asset, e.g. 'Welcome Email #3 - Value Story'.

path.description: Relative path from the project root to the asset file, e.g. './campaigns/spring-launch/emails/03-value-story.md'.

notes.description: Any additional context about this asset.

voice_profile_ref.description: Path to the voice profile used for this campaign, typically './brand/voice-profile.md'. Can be overridden per-campaign if a different voice is needed.

positioning_ref.description: Path to the positioning file, typically './brand/positioning.md'.

voice_notes.description: Campaign-specific voice adjustments. E.g., 'More urgent tone for flash sale. Shorter sentences. More exclamation points than usual.'

results.description: Performance data for the campaign. Filled in during or after the campaign runs.

summary.description: One-paragraph summary of how the campaign performed.

metric.description: Name of the metric, e.g. 'Open Rate', 'CTR', 'Signups', 'Revenue'.

target.description: What the target was, if set, e.g. '500 signups'.

learnings.description: What was learned from this campaign. These should also be appended to ./brand/learnings.md.

created_by.description: The skill that created this brief, e.g. '/email-sequences' or '/lead-magnet'.
